extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping palpitations from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. Palpitations appears in only one rule, r2 (priority 100, the highest), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest acuity) when rash AND leg_swelling AND slurred_speech AND palpitations are all true.

Consider the input rash=true, leg_swelling=true, slurred_speech=true, chest_pain=false, syncope=false.

With palpitations=FALSE: r2 does not fire (palpitations missing). Matching rules: r3 (leg_swelling, priority 3) -> emergent; r4 (rash, priority 2) -> urgent; r6 (slurred_speech, priority 2) -> urgent. Highest-priority match is r3 (priority 3), so acuity = emergent (highest level).

With palpitations=TRUE (all else identical): r2 now matches (all four conjuncts true). Its priority 100 beats r3 (priority 3) and the priority-2 rules, so r2 wins and acuity = self_care (lowest level).

Thus setting palpitations true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, the maximum possible decrease. This violates monotonicity. The witness assignment given is the palpitations=true side of the counterexample; the comparison is against the same assignment with palpitations=false.
